Notes: Some 4th, maybe isolated 5th by snoqualmie standards. Crux for me was honestly the last pinnacle along the summit ridge, but I was chatting with some random college kid who was sitting on top while I pulled it which made the whole thing less stressful.
Location: Washington > Central-West Cascades & Seattle > Snoqualmie Pass Area > Guye Peak > Summer-Fall (rock)

Notes: Worked it on TR solo, I linked the bottom three bolts but couldn’t reach the edge just above the third bolt. I think that would’ve been the end of the hard climbing RIP
Location: Oregon > Mt. Hood National Forest > French's Dome

Notes: Felt tired today, no really hard moves but very few easy ones. Pump got to me at bolt 4. Wipped and smacked kendall in the face RIP
Location: Oregon > Mt. Hood National Forest > French's Dome

Notes: Did this to rig TR solo on the dark side, it was so mobbed. Wore a backpack up which made things more interesting.
Location: Oregon > Mt. Hood National Forest > French's Dome

Notes: Scrambled up the first 5.7 pitch to fix a rope to TR solo some of the routes below the ledge. Properly 5.7 I’d say
Location: Washington > South-West & Tacoma > Columbia Gorge > Beacon Rock > Northwest Face

Notes: TR, I should’ve tried to lead on gear. I saw a guy struggle above nested .1s on this before me though and got scared. At least I never weighted the rope!
Location: Washington > South-West & Tacoma > Columbia Gorge > Beacon Rock > Northwest Face

Notes: TR solo P1, hung at one of the cruxes. Super fun!
Location: Washington > South-West & Tacoma > Columbia Gorge > Beacon Rock > Northwest Face

Notes: TR solo, so so fun !! The hand crack was such a blast but only like two moves
Location: Washington > South-West & Tacoma > Columbia Gorge > Beacon Rock > Northwest Face

Notes: Followed first, lead second. Second pitch Felt less like a route and more like doing V1-V3 boulders between ledges. Aided the crux :/
Location: Washington > South-West & Tacoma > Columbia Gorge > Beacon Rock > Northwest Face

Notes: Lead the first pitch, probably 10c? Kinda run out but above two solid nested pieces
Location: Washington > South-West & Tacoma > Columbia Gorge > Beacon Rock > Northwest Face

Notes: Simul w/ Aiden, we were bottom to top in a little over 2 hours. Simul system is still in the getting dialed process, should have had both climbers take coils so the leader could drop coils if they were going faster. Too much ledge walking, and very very softly graded climbing. Bike ride off was a total treat though!!
Location: Washington > Okanogan > Mazama > Goat Wall

Notes: With Aiden! Such a fun romp, we goofed off so much. Kinda regret not doing the MJ variation. No one else on the route was v lucky. The button heads on P3 were the highlight of the route for me, I clipped them for giggles. Wonder if anyone has ever whipped
Location: Washington > Central-East Cascades, Wenatchee, & Leavenworth > Leavenworth > Icicle Creek > Snow Creek Area > Snow Creek Wall

Notes:
Idk how many times I’ve climbed this but did it again today! I always forget how annoying the WTTJ approach is, me and all my homies hate mossy talus. pitch 1 felt a little harder than normal, but more secure than I remember. pitch 2 felt the easiest it ever has, rest of it is a cake walk as always, although the P3 moves were more exposed than i remembered which was awesome!


Also I feel like I really remember this being 5.9 bc of the pocket move on the P1 traverse but maybe I made that up?
Location: Washington > Central-West Cascades & Seattle > North Bend & Vicinity > Exit 38 > Far Side > Interstate Park > (a) Headlight Point

Notes: With aiden! Climbing with friends is mega fun. Total jug haul, but some interesting moves. There’s actually 4 pitches, with the 3rd being bushy 3rd class, and the 4th being a short trad protected 5.6 (probably a 1-3 would sew it).
Location: Washington > Central-West Cascades & Seattle > North Bend & Vicinity > Exit 38 > Far Side > Sunshine Vista (Easy Street)

Notes: Fun romp, a little hot out for my taste. Some of the slab feet were a little mossy but there are mega jug ledges pretty much everywhere you look.
Location: Washington > Central-West Cascades & Seattle > North Bend & Vicinity > Exit 34; Middle Fork & Taylor River > Garfield Ledges > Spouse Wall

Notes: Meh route, setting a TR for a newer climber. Annoying anchor sitch w/ all the culture shock traffic. I do not like never mind at all.
Location: Washington > Central-West Cascades & Seattle > North Bend & Vicinity > Exit 38 > Deception Crags > Nevermind Wall

Notes: Lead b4 doing no rope. As with most grit scone routes more of a high ball than a route
Location: Washington > Central-West Cascades & Seattle > North Bend & Vicinity > Exit 38 > Far Side > Gritscone

Notes: Actually pretty fun. Last route I hadn’t climbed on the grit scone. Someone cleaned it up recently so the holds are super nice! Thx
Location: Washington > Central-West Cascades & Seattle > North Bend & Vicinity > Exit 38 > Far Side > Gritscone

Notes: Uhhhh this happened. RIP Cameron’s car. Praying to the gods of SPD
Location: Washington > Central-West Cascades & Seattle > North Bend & Vicinity > Exit 34; Middle Fork & Taylor River > Mount Garfield

Notes: TR solo, two hangs, couldn’t figure out the last move before bumping into the jug rail.
Location: Washington > Central-West Cascades & Seattle > North Bend & Vicinity > Exit 32; Little Si > Canopy Crag Group > (b) Canopy Crag

Notes: TR solo, hung twice. Super fun
Location: Washington > Central-West Cascades & Seattle > North Bend & Vicinity > Exit 32; Little Si > Canopy Crag Group > (b) Canopy Crag

Notes: TR solo, had to French free the crux, I don’t think I can climb this hard
Location: Washington > Central-West Cascades & Seattle > North Bend & Vicinity > Exit 32; Little Si > Canopy Crag Group > (b) Canopy Crag

Notes: TR solo. Got it first go. Maybe going to start claiming I can climb 5.11 maybe
Location: Washington > Central-West Cascades & Seattle > North Bend & Vicinity > Exit 32; Little Si > Canopy Crag Group > (b) Canopy Crag

Notes: TR solo send! I tried it like 5 times before. One of the hardest climbs grade wise I’ve done technically? Although it’s really just the opening boulder followed by exit climbing. Also TR so not really
Location: Washington > Central-West Cascades & Seattle > North Bend & Vicinity > Exit 32; Little Si > Canopy Crag Group > (b) Canopy Crag

Notes: Followed first pitch, lead the second, third was wet and the sun was getting low so we skipped it. Hung once on p2, v pumpy
Location: Washington > Central-West Cascades & Seattle > Skykomish Valley > Index > Lookout Point Area > Rattletale Wall

Notes: Reports about this being sandbagged are absurd. 5.8 at the hardest imo
Location: Washington > Central-West Cascades & Seattle > North Bend & Vicinity > Exit 34; Middle Fork & Taylor River > Stegosaurus Butte > The Rump > The Butte-tresses

Notes: Cracks were pretty mungy but very fun overall. Last 20 feet or so were a delight
Location: Washington > Central-West Cascades & Seattle > North Bend & Vicinity > Exit 34; Middle Fork & Taylor River > Stegosaurus Butte > The Rump > The Goods

Route: I Can't Believe It's Not Butte-r
Notes: The 5.9 corner at the start of P4 was the crux for me, but went pretty easy after I chimney climbed until the crack widened to 1s
Location: Washington > Central-West Cascades & Seattle > North Bend & Vicinity > Exit 34; Middle Fork & Taylor River > Stegosaurus Butte > The Rump > The Butte-tresses

Notes: Did the .10b direct start (I think it’s called RMP?). The 5.6 pitch into it was a little confusing route finding wise, aim for red tat on a bolted anchor. Followed the .10b pitch, hung once on crux, so did my partner who led it. Rest of the route was a fun romp.
Location: Washington > Central-East Cascades, Wenatchee, & Leavenworth > Leavenworth > Icicle Creek > Snow Creek Area > Snow Creek Wall

Notes: The 5.10 pitches are unbelievably fun, stoked that I got to lead both. Graciously let my partner lead the 5.9 OW grovel. The 5.8 top pitch also amazing. We topped out the route and walked down via gunsight notch, would not recommend. I broke a foothold on the exit pitch while maybe 50 feet runout above a placement in bad rock. However climbing is only maybe 5.6 at the absolute hardest on that pitch.
Location: Nevada > Southern Nevada > Red Rocks > Juniper Canyon > Brownstone Wall

Notes: I think I should probably tick inti instead (we did inti then finished with the last 9 pitches of res arete), but doing the res arete finish makes for a very different day. Hung once following crux pitch and once before doing a long-ish 5.7 runout on pitch 6/7(??? Don’t remember exactly). Route finding for res arete finish is trivial if you use the Steph abegg beta
Location: Nevada > Southern Nevada > Red Rocks > Mount Wilson > Aeolian Wall

Notes: Fun weekend romp with Ethan. We were planning on doing steamboat captains dream but everything was soaking wet. Soaking wet 5.9 is p hard
Location: Washington > South-West & Tacoma > Columbia Gorge > Beacon Rock > Northwest Face

Notes: Leading women in comfortable shoes was really not fun first thing in the morning. I don’t like slab climbing. I hung more times than I can count.
Location: International > North America > Canada > British Columbia > Squamish > Stawamus Chief > The Bulletheads > Bulletheads South

Notes: Holy blast of a route Batman! Fun, rompy climbing, graded like it’s an exits climb, interesting and generally overhung pitches. The MHC rap beta was pretty weird for us to follow but if you just rap straight down MHC the anchors appear
Location: Washington > Northwest Region > Darrington and Mountain Loop Hwy > Glacier Peak Wilderness > Morning Star Peak

Notes: Meh. A good amount of loose rock. Fun adventurous day but wouldn’t recommend pitching any of it out, just simul from the base. We pitched out the first 6(?) pitches and it was unnecessary
Location: Washington > Central-West Cascades & Seattle > North Bend & Vicinity > Exit 34; Middle Fork & Taylor River > Quartz Mountain
New York > Adirondacks > Chapel Pond Pass > Chapel Pond Slab
New York > Adirondacks > Keene Region > Pitchoff Chimney Cliff
